title: NGE22 questions comments
wikitext:
[]: NGE22' questions/comments             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 25, 2007 3:59 pm Holy crap, the forums are back up! Nukie wrote: One more question-were the drawings shown after the strangulation scene the same ones shown in Asuka's mind rape?

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sat Jan 27, 2007 2:22 am i never really got what those pictures meant...it reminds me of Silent Hill 3...awsome games BTW

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 27, 2007 7:17 am looks like a house on fire, a male and female (boy and girl? or mother and father>) bleeding all over the place and they've got knives in their hands (they kill each other or they kill themselves?), and a mean, pissed off person.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Jan 27, 2007 10:04 am I read somewhere that those are pictures drawn by children who had suffered real-life abusive or traumatic experiences.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:14 pm If they aren't the real thing, then they are pretty good facsimiles. My first thoughts on seeing those images was that Anno had been raiding other peoples' art therapy as input to his own.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 27, 2007 2:15 pm Regardless of WHAT the pics represent, the fact that their from after the Asuka stranglation scene in EoE and inserted into the 22' Asuka mindrape scene must symbolize something. Don't ask me what, though. lol I quote meself as nobody answered this ? yet wrote: I thought it was interesting because in 22' during the mindrape scene when Asuka claims "I can live alone! I don't need anybody!" one of the images that flash by is from episode 25/26 when Asuka is standing behind Shinji in his mental prison. :shock: I guess it has to do with the fact that those DC scenes were added into the episodes AFTER End of Evangelion was already released?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 27, 2007 2:24 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: inserted into the 22' Asuka mindrape scene must symbolize something.
